methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by James Coutre. The comparable universe for this sector consists of 7 private companies—including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—with no public peers available for direct comparison.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While 5 companies operate within the Drones & Autonomous sector, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comps yields fewer than 3 datapoints in any filtered subset. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital efficiency across drone and autonomous platforms. No alternative triangulation methods were supported by available data.

The sparse and heterogeneous comp landscape creates a significant constraint on precision. A defensible valuation range cannot be derived from the current data without resorting to methodologies outside the peer-median framework. Any valuation would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set to include adjacent sectors or earlier-stage fundraising rounds from existing peers; or (2) application of cost-of-capital or sum-of-the-parts methods, neither of which is supported by the provided dataset. Key uncertainties include the depth of technical differentiation, capital efficiency relative to later-stage peers, and product-market fit validation.
